source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Thermal burn injury from ruptured sight glass
event.incident_type: Other Injury - Burns to the body
actions_taken: Operator immediately shut down the system and closed the suction valve
Operator proceeded to emergency wash station and stood under flowing water for several minutes
Rig's OIM and Medic were notified
Operator was examined by rig's Medic
Medevac helicopter was notified and operator was transported to inland facility for further treatment
event.summary: On November 7, 2015, an Engine Room Operator sustained first and second degree burns on approximately 40% of his body when an inspection glass on a Marine Sanitation Device (MSD) vacuum pump ruptured, ejecting hot fluids and fractured glass. The operator was troubleshooting an alarm on the MSD when the incident occurred. The direct cause was excessive scale buildup in the discharge line causing overpressurization of the pump, as no pressure relief valve or rupture disk was installed to prevent overpressuring.
recommendations: Install pressure relief valves or rupture disks on MSD equipment to prevent overpressuring
Install pressure and temperature gauges on equipment to assist crew during inspection and troubleshooting
Develop formal procedures for troubleshooting MSD alarms
Ensure manufacturer's Operations Manual is available to crew
Redesign equipment to prevent operator body placement in line of fire from inspection glass

threats.name: Scale buildup in discharge line causing overpressurization
Operator proximity to pressurized component during troubleshooting

consequences.name: Inspection glass rupture and ejection of hot fluids

controls.name: Pressure Relief Valve
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent pressure buildup

supporting_text: there was no safety device in place to prevent overpressuring; such as, a Pressure Relief Valve or a Rupture Disk

controls.name: Rupture Disk
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent pressure buildup

supporting_text: there was no safety device in place to prevent overpressuring; such as, a Pressure Relief Valve or a Rupture Disk

controls.name: Pressure and Temperature Gauges
controls.barrier_role: Enable early detection of system overpressure and overheating
controls.lod_basis: Instrumentation to detect abnormal conditions before failure

supporting_text: the equipment doesn't have any pressure or temperature gauges installed to assist crew members when inspecting or troubleshooting the system

controls.name: Formal MSD Troubleshooting Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Establish systematic approach to troubleshooting to identify root causes before taking action
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent reactive troubleshooting without diagnosis

supporting_text: No formal process or procedure was in place onboard the vessel for troubleshooting the alarms on the Marine Sanitation Device
It was common practice for the crew to reset the alarm and restart the pump of the MSD before first identifying the cause

controls.name: Manufacturer's Operations Manual Availability
controls.barrier_role: Provide crew with equipment-specific guidance for operation and troubleshooting
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure access to technical documentation

supporting_text: Manufacturer's 'Operations Manuel' was not available to the crew at the time of the incident

controls.name: Safe Work Positioning for Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Prevent operator exposure to pressurized component discharge
controls.lod_basis: Design control to eliminate operator placement in line of fire

supporting_text: Body Placement: In order to see through the inspection glass, operator must place his/her body in the line of fire
The IP arrived at the inspection glass and kneeled down next to the pump in order to gain a clear view of the inside of the pump

controls.name: Emergency Wash Station
controls.barrier_role: Mitigate thermal injury severity by immediate decontamination and cooling
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to minimize injury consequences

supporting_text: The IP proceeded to the nearest emergency wash station because he was covered in sewage and his skin was burning. He undressed and stood under the flowing water for several minutes in an attempt to wash his skin free of contaminants and help to slow down the burning process

controls.name: Rig Medical Facility and Medevac
controls.barrier_role: Provide emergency medical treatment and transport to definitive care
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to provide medical intervention

supporting_text: After washing off all the contaminants, the IP notified the rig's OIM and Medic about what had occured. IP was examined by the rig's Medic and the decision was made to send the IP to an inland facility for further treatment. At approximately 21:37 hours the Medevac Helicopter was notified of the emergency

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
